testcase.name: Intel Tab — Navigation › clicking Intel tab activates it and shows Intel Sections sidebar
Intel Tab — Navigation › keyboard shortcut 9 activates Intel tab
Intel Tab — Navigation › sidebar shows all 5 sections: Briefings, Correlations, Market, Roasters, Anomalies
Intel Tab — Navigation › Quick Stats shows "28 roasters monitored" after data loads
Intel Tab — Briefings View › Briefings is the default section shown on tab activation
Intel Tab — Briefings View › Generate Daily and Generate Weekly buttons are visible
Intel Tab — Briefings View › empty state message appears when no briefings exist
Intel Tab — Briefings View › empty state sub-message instructs user to generate a briefing
Intel Tab — Correlations View › "Cross-Signal Correlations" heading is visible
Intel Tab — Correlations View › "Refresh" button is present
Intel Tab — Correlations View › empty state shows when no correlations exist
Intel Tab — Market View › "Market Intelligence" heading is visible
Intel Tab — Market View › "Product Catalogue" summary card is present
Intel Tab — Market View › "Outlier Alerts" summary card is present
Intel Tab — Market View › "Google Trends" summary card is present
Intel Tab — Market View › "View Products" button is present
Intel Tab — Market View › exactly 3 summary cards are shown in Market view
Intel Tab — Roasters View › "28 active" roasters count label is shown
Intel Tab — Roasters View › all 6 scrape buttons are visible: Homepages, Catalogues, Social, Trends, EDMs, Log
Intel Tab — Roasters View › scrape status bar shows HP, Cat, Social, Trends, EDM schedule fields
Intel Tab — Roasters View › roaster table has all required column headers
Intel Tab — Roasters View › roaster table has 28 data rows
Intel Tab — Roasters View › each roaster row has an HP button and an AI button
Intel Tab — Roasters View › first roaster row shows a known roaster name
Intel Tab — Roasters View › scrape status bar shows "idle" or "running" state indicator
Intel Tab — Anomalies View › "KPI Anomalies" heading is visible
Intel Tab — Anomalies View › empty state shows when no anomalies are detected
Intel Tab — Anomalies View › empty state sub-message is informative
Intel Tab — Sidebar Navigation Flow › can navigate through all 5 sections sequentially
Intel Tab — Sidebar Navigation Flow › active sidebar item is highlighted when switching sections
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/roasters returns 200 with roasters array of count 28
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/roasters — each roaster has required fields
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/correlations returns 200 with correlations array
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/trends returns 200
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/overview returns 200 with roasters, products, briefings, anomalies keys
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/overview — roasters count is 28
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/briefings?limit=5 returns 200 with briefings array
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/products?aggregate=true returns 200
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/internal/anomalies returns 200 with anomalies array
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/scrape/cibe-status returns 200 with schedule timestamps
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/scrape/cibe-status — schedule has all 5 frequency fields
CIBE API Endpoints › GET /api/cibe/scrape/cibe-status — nextHomepage is a valid ISO date string
